chapters.summary: Units, dimensions, significant figures, error analysis — 1-2 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: SI units, fundamental and derived units — frequently tested in conceptual 1-mark questions.
Least count calculation, zero error correction — directly linked to Experimental Skills unit.
Absolute, relative, percentage error; error propagation in derived quantities.
Rules for significant figures in measurement and calculations.
Checking dimensional consistency, deriving relationships, converting units.

chapters.summary: Motion in straight line, projectile, relative velocity, uniform circular motion — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Position-time and velocity-time graphs; equations of uniformly accelerated motion.
Vector addition, subtraction, scalar and vector products, unit vector, resolution of vectors.
Relative velocity in 1D and 2D — river-boat, rain-man problems.
Time of flight, maximum height, range, angle of projection — very frequently tested.
Angular velocity, centripetal acceleration, relationship between linear and angular quantities.

chapters.summary: Newton's laws, friction, circular motion dynamics — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: First, Second (F=ma), Third law; impulse-momentum theorem; law of conservation of linear momentum.
Lami's theorem, resolution of forces — static equilibrium problems.
Static and kinetic friction, laws of friction, rolling friction; motion on inclined planes.
Centripetal force; vehicle on level road; vehicle on banked road — both formula and concept tested.

chapters.summary: Work-energy theorem, conservation of energy, collisions — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Work done by constant and variable forces; kinetic energy; work-energy theorem applications.
Potential energy of spring; conservative vs non-conservative forces; potential energy curves.
Energy conservation in systems; motion in a vertical circle.
Elastic and inelastic collisions in 1D and 2D; coefficient of restitution.
Definition of power, average and instantaneous power, units.

chapters.summary: Moment of inertia, torque, angular momentum, rolling motion — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Centre of mass of two-particle system and rigid bodies; motion of centre of mass.
Moment of a force; torque; angular momentum; conservation of angular momentum and applications.
Radius of gyration; MI of simple geometrical objects (rod, disc, sphere, ring); parallel and perpendicular axes theorems.
Conditions for translational and rotational equilibrium; couple.
Pure rolling; equations of rotational motion; comparison with linear motion; rolling on inclined planes.

chapters.summary: Kepler's laws, gravitational potential, satellites — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Newton's law of gravitation; gravitational constant G.
Variation of g with altitude, depth, latitude; g on other planets.
Laws of orbits, areas, and periods; areal velocity; T² ∝ R³.
Gravitational potential; potential energy in multi-body systems; binding energy.
Orbital velocity; time period and energy of satellite; geostationary orbit; escape velocity.

chapters.summary: Elasticity, viscosity, surface tension, thermal properties — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines solids, liquids AND thermal properties in one unit.

topics.explanation: Elastic behaviour; stress-strain relationship; Hooke's law; Young's modulus, bulk modulus, modulus of rigidity.
Pressure due to fluid column; Pascal's law; effect of gravity on fluid pressure; hydraulic machines.
Viscosity; Stokes' law; terminal velocity; streamline vs turbulent flow; critical velocity; Reynolds number.
Bernoulli's theorem; Venturi meter; dynamic lift; applications.
Surface energy; angle of contact; excess pressure in drops and bubbles; capillary rise.
Heat and temperature; thermal expansion of solids, liquids, gases; specific heat capacity; calorimetry.

topics.topicName: Change of State and Latent Heat
topics.explanation: Melting, boiling, sublimation; latent heat of fusion and vaporisation.
Conduction (thermal conductivity); convection; radiation (Stefan's law, Wien's law, Newton's law of cooling).

chapters.summary: Laws of thermodynamics, heat engines — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.topicName: Zeroth and First Law of Thermodynamics
topics.explanation: Thermal equilibrium; zeroth law; first law: ΔU = Q + W; internal energy; heat and work in thermodynamic processes.
Isothermal, adiabatic, isochoric, isobaric processes; PV diagrams; work done in each process.
Second law of thermodynamics; reversible and irreversible processes; Carnot engine; efficiency = 1 - T₂/T₁.

chapters.summary: Ideal gas, degrees of freedom, equipartition theorem — 1- 2 questions annually in NEET.

topics.topicName: Ideal Gas Equation and Kinetic Theory
topics.explanation: PV = nRT; kinetic theory assumptions; pressure of ideal gas; RMS speed; kinetic interpretation of temperature.
Degrees of freedom for mono/di/polyatomic gases; law of equipartition of energy; Cv, Cp, γ values.
Mean free path concept; Avogadro's number.

chapters.summary: SHM, wave motion, superposition, Doppler effect — 4-5 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: SHM equation; phase; energy in SHM (KE and PE); simple pendulum derivation; spring-mass system.
Restoring force; force constant; time period of spring combinations (series and parallel).
Longitudinal and transverse waves; speed of wave; displacement equation of progressive wave.
Principle of superposition; reflection of waves; standing waves in strings and organ pipes; fundamental mode and harmonics; Beats.
Apparent frequency for source and observer in motion; all four cases and combined formula.

chapters.summary: Coulomb's law, Gauss's law, potential, capacitance — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Coulomb's law; superposition principle; electric field due to point charge; electric field lines.
Electric field and potential due to a dipole; torque on dipole in uniform electric field.
Electric flux; Gauss's law; field due to infinite line charge, infinite plane sheet, spherical shell.
Electric potential due to point charge, dipole, system of charges; potential difference; equipotential surfaces.
PE of system of two point charges; PE of dipole in electrostatic field.
Conductors and insulators; dielectrics and electric polarisation; capacitors in series and parallel; parallel plate capacitor with and without dielectric; energy stored in capacitor.

chapters.summary: Ohm's law, Kirchhoff's laws, Wheatstone bridge, potentiometer — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Electric current; drift velocity; mobility; Ohm's law; V-I characteristics (ohmic and non-ohmic); electrical resistivity and conductivity; temperature dependence of resistance.
Electrical energy; power; series and parallel combination of resistors.
Internal resistance; potential difference vs EMF; cells in series and parallel.
KCL and KVL; applications to complex networks; Wheatstone bridge (balanced and unbalanced).
Metre bridge for measuring unknown resistance and specific resistance; potentiometer for comparing EMFs and finding internal resistance.

chapters.chapterName: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
chapters.summary: Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, Lorentz force, magnetic materials — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines current magnetism and magnetic materials into one unit.

topics.explanation: Magnetic field due to current-carrying circular loop; straight wire; solenoid using Biot-Savart and Ampere's law.
Ampere's circuital law; field due to infinitely long straight wire; solenoid and toroid.

topics.topicName: Lorentz Force and Charged Particle Motion
topics.explanation: Force on moving charge in E and B fields; circular motion in magnetic field; cyclotron.
Force on conductor in magnetic field; force between two parallel current-carrying conductors; definition of ampere.
Torque on current loop; galvanometer sensitivity; conversion to ammeter and voltmeter.
Bar magnet as equivalent solenoid; magnetic field of a dipole; torque on dipole; para-, dia-, ferromagnetism; effect of temperature on magnetic properties; Earth's magnetism.

chapters.summary: Faraday's law, Lenz's law, LCR circuits, transformer — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines EMI and AC into one unit.

topics.explanation: Magnetic flux; Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction; Lenz's law; motional EMF; eddy currents.
Self-inductance of a coil; mutual inductance; energy stored in inductor.
AC voltage and current; peak and RMS values; reactance and impedance; phasor representation.
Impedance of LCR; resonance frequency; Q-factor; power in AC circuits; power factor; wattless current.
Working principle of AC generator; step-up and step-down transformers; transformer equation; energy losses.

chapters.summary: EM spectrum, displacement current — 1 question annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Concept of displacement current; Ampere-Maxwell law.
Characteristics of EM waves; transverse nature; EM spectrum: radio, microwave, infrared, visible, UV, X-rays, gamma rays — wavelength ranges and applications.

chapters.summary: Ray optics, wave optics, optical instruments — 4-5 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines ray and wave optics into one unit.

topics.explanation: Laws of reflection; spherical mirrors; mirror formula; sign convention; magnification.
Refraction at plane and spherical surfaces; thin lens formula; lens maker's formula; power of lens; combination of lenses.
Critical angle; TIR applications: optical fibre, prism, mirage.
Refraction through prism; angle of deviation vs angle of incidence; minimum deviation; dispersion of white light.
Simple microscope, compound microscope, reflecting and refracting telescopes (astronomical); magnifying power and image formation.
Wavefront; Huygens' principle; laws of reflection and refraction using Huygens' principle.
Coherent sources; sustained interference; YDSE: fringe width formula, path difference, positions of bright and dark fringes.
Single slit diffraction; width of central maximum.
Plane polarised light; Brewster's law; Malus' law; uses of plane polarised light and Polaroid.

chapters.chapterName: Dual Nature of Matter and Radiation
chapters.summary: Photoelectric effect, de Broglie wavelength — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Hertz and Lenard's observations; Einstein's photoelectric equation; work function; threshold frequency; stopping potential — very commonly tested numerically.
Matter waves; de Broglie relation λ = h/mv; Davisson-Germer experiment (experimental evidence).

chapters.summary: Bohr model, hydrogen spectrum, nuclear physics — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines Atoms and Nuclei into one unit.

topics.explanation: Alpha-particle scattering; Rutherford nuclear model; Bohr model postulates; energy levels and radii of hydrogen atom.
Spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen, Brackett, Pfund); wavelength formula; transitions.
Nuclear composition; atomic mass; mass-energy relation (E=mc²); mass defect.
Binding energy per nucleon; BE-mass number curve; nuclear fission and fusion.
Alpha, beta, gamma decay; radioactive decay law; half-life; mean life; activity.

chapters.summary: Semiconductors, diodes, logic gates — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors; p-type and n-type; energy bands.
p-n junction; forward and reverse bias; I-V characteristics; diode as rectifier (half-wave and full-wave).
I-V characteristics of LED, photodiode, solar cell; Zener diode characteristics and as voltage regulator.
OR, AND, NOT, NAND, NOR gates; truth tables; Boolean expressions; universal gates.

chapters.summary: Lab-based practical observations — 1-2 questions annually in NEET. Tests conceptual understanding of experiments.

topics.explanation: Using Vernier calipers for internal/external diameter and depth; screw gauge for thin sheet/wire thickness.
Dissipation of energy — graph of (amplitude)² vs time.
Elasticity of metallic wire.
Surface tension by capillary rise; coefficient of viscosity by terminal velocity.
Speed of sound using resonance tube; specific heat capacity by method of mixtures.
Resistivity using metre bridge; resistance using Ohm's law; galvanometer by half-deflection method.
Focal length of convex/concave mirror and convex lens (parallax method); angle of deviation vs angle of incidence for prism; refractive index of glass slab.
I-V characteristics of p-n junction diode and Zener diode; identification of diode, LED, resistor, capacitor from mixed collection.
